title: Finishing the twin - DRM.jl + drmTMB
subtitle: Evidence-led mission control for the DRM.jl <-> drmTMB twin, now running R-first: native drmTMB support/status first, Julia acceleration and parity second. A slice is not done unless engine support, R bridge support, point estimates, CI/status, tests, docs/articles, visuals, issue evidence, and Rose audit agree.

slices.name: Standardize interval statuses across summary, confint, diagnostics, and bridge payloads
Promote Gaussian sd:mu:phylo target only with point, logLik, profile, bootstrap, refit-count, and failure-reason parity
Require no speed headline without point-estimate and inference-status evidence
Add native R/TMB, R-to-Julia, and direct Julia comparison rows for promoted cells
Random slopes: fixed-effect likelihoods, independent slopes, correlated slopes, then structured slopes
Non-Gaussian: coefficient parity before variance/correlation and CI claims
Bivariate rho12: keep residual correlation separate from structured and group-level correlations
Mixed and cross-family models: keep Gaussian plus non-Gaussian correlations experimental until recovery and CI evidence pass
High-q correlations: q4 first, q8 second, higher only after transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status gates
Structural dependencies: animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE provenance/recovery/bridge/docs/visual gates
Benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl as separate routes
Separate cold start, warm start, marshalling, Julia kernel, R reconstruction, profile batches, and bootstrap batches
Borrow HSquared AI-REML lessons only for exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells
Use observed-information Newton, Fisher/natural-gradient, exact AD/implicit gradients, or Takahashi/selected-inverse methods only after derivation
Record estimates, logLik/objective, CI endpoints/status, convergence, versions, threads, BLAS/OpenMP, memory, and dirty flags
Stream long benchmark partial rows without overwriting full artifacts
Keep GPU/backend vocabulary planned until evidence exists
Implement observed-response masks as likelihood/FIML-style handling, not mean imputation
Complete-data all-true mask equals current complete-data logLik exactly
Bivariate partial-pair behaviour documented and tested
Missing-response bridge passes R-to-Julia-to-R for advertised families
Missing-predictor policy is explicit and separate from response masks
Missingness heatmap, diagnostics, article, parity tests, and issue links

phases.name: Visuals and articles built as we go

slices.name: Capability heatmap, R-Julia parity plot, profile-likelihood curve, and Wald/profile/bootstrap comparison
Missingness heatmap, phylo/tree covariance, spatial field, kernel matrix, recovery, and runtime-plus-CI plots
Articles for overview, Gaussian baseline, non-Gaussian, random slopes, rho12, mixed-family, structures, missing values, bridge, CIs, benchmarks, and limits
Pat and Darwin applied-user walkthrough before tutorial promotion
Florence, Fisher, Hopper, Grace, and Rose review before done language
Visit GLLVM.jl/gllvmTMB before bridge, mixed-family, missingness, structural, and benchmark promotion
slices.evidence: GLLVM finish plan reviewed and dashboard lessons imported

slices.name: Visit HSquared.jl/hsquared before REML/AI-style, variance-component CI, matrix, and dashboard promotion
Write scout note or issue comment for each imported idea
Borrow patterns, not claims; Rose checks borrowed terminology
Start big simulations only after smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass
ADEMP grids cover Gaussian, non-Gaussian, mixed-family, random slopes, high-q, missingness, structures, and bridge cells
Coverage reports include usable-CI denominators and MCSE
Comparator checks use same-estimand targets and record unavailable comparators honestly
Simulation outputs feed articles, visuals, dashboard, and issue evidence
R side: devtools::document(), devtools::test(), devtools::check(), pkgdown::check_pkgdown()
Julia side: Pkg.test(), Documenter build, benchmark and parity artifacts
Release notes separate local health, public CI/pkgdown, speed evidence, unsupported cells, and validation debt
Issue ledger cleanup and final dashboard freeze
Rose signoff before release-ready language
CRAN submission
slices.evidence: No CRAN submission without explicit user decision

repos.name: drmTMB
repos.head: serve-time
repos.note: R-first Ayumi branch. Repairs Julia q4 target estimates from phylocov and banks a tiny all-q4 bootstrap smoke before any 10k claim.

repos.name: DRM.jl
repos.head: 9bdea65
repos.note: DRM.jl #292 merged green; verified through /tmp/DRM-main-merged. The user's saved DRM.jl checkout may still be dirty.

repos.name: gllvmTMB
repos.head: f046d0f
repos.note: Sister R bridge; gate-drift mirror tracked in gllvmTMB#488.

repos.name: GLLVM.jl
repos.head: 6d8e158
repos.note: Template source for finish-plan dashboard, bridge matrix, and benchmark discipline.

repos.name: hsquared
repos.note: R package model for capability/status discipline and REML CI language.

repos.name: HSquared.jl
repos.head: feadbd8
repos.note: AI-REML and selected-inverse lessons are design analogues, not transferred claims.

matrix.next: Keep status.json, sweep.json, check-log, and after-task notes synchronized with issues.
Wire README, ROADMAP, NEWS, pkgdown, Documenter, and dashboard claims to this matrix.
Implement drmTMB#544 generated gate-vs-engine audit and CI guard.
Promote only after native R, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct Julia point estimate plus CI/status parity are recorded in one row.
Fixed-effect likelihoods first, independent slopes second, correlated slopes third, structured slopes last.
Coefficient parity first; variance, correlation, and CI claims require their own recovery rows.
Keep residual rho12 separate from group, phylogenetic, spatial, kernel, and cross-family correlations.
Use DRM.jl#280-style recovery and bridge labels before user-facing promotion.
q4 first, q8 second; higher q requires transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status evidence.
Animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE rows each need provenance, PSD/name-alignment tests, recovery, bridge parity, docs, and visuals.
No speed headline without point-estimate and CI/status evidence; benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl separately.
Borrow hsquared only as a design analogue for exact Gaussian MME cells; use the actual method name for Laplace/non-Gaussian cells.
Use likelihood/FIML-style masks; complete-data all-true masks must match current complete-data log-likelihood exactly.
Every major capability needs a real visual: heatmap, profile curve, parity plot, missingness heatmap, structural visual, and runtime-plus-CI plot where relevant.
Big simulations start only after smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass.
Release notes must separate local package health, public CI/pkgdown state, speed evidence, unsupported cells, and remaining validation debt. No CRAN submission without user decision.

active_work.text: R-first pivot is active: native drmTMB status and support diagnostics are being hardened before broader Julia speed claims.
Julia q4 target-estimate repair is merged as #565 and the dashboard evidence-link copy fix is merged as #566; the live board is serving from /tmp/drm-dashboard on port 8765.
Ayumi Julia q4 point-fit bridge blocker is fixed, target estimates now come from Sigma_a, ML all-q4 bootstrap has a B=2 plumbing smoke, and 10k wall-time remains unclaimed.

Native TMB ML bootstrap is not yet a fallback claim: careful optimizer settings give 2/2 refits at 30 tips, but 100-tip careful and robust smokes still return bootstrap_unavailable after both refits fail.

activity.text: Added native-TMB optimizer-preset controls to the Ayumi q4 harness; careful bootstrap succeeds at 30 tips, but both careful and robust 100-tip smokes still fail both refits.
drmTMB#565 banked the Julia q4 phylocov target-estimate repair; 30-tip Ayumi Julia ML all-q4 bootstrap B=2 returned four rows with 2/2 refits.
drmTMB#566 fixed served-dashboard evidence links by copying all after-task notes into /tmp/drm-dashboard.
Native TMB ML q4 bootstrap 30-tip and 100-tip Ayumi smokes returned bootstrap_unavailable for all four phylogenetic SD rows; keep native bootstrap fallback wording conservative.
Internal issue ledger updated with the Julia bootstrap plumbing success and native bootstrap negative evidence.
DRM.jl#292 merged the q4 bridge-vcov fix after docs and Julia test jobs passed; drmTMB#555 and DRM.jl#291 now carry the merged-main smoke evidence.
drmTMB#556 merged the R-side Julia REML requested/effective estimator status truth table after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#557 banked native TMB REML rejection-neighbour coverage, drmTMB#558 banked Julia confint target wording, and drmTMB#559 banked the Ayumi q4 support/status harness.
drmTMB#552/#553 banked native q4 ML profile row-status handling for sigma-axis phylogenetic SD targets.
drmTMB#549 merged the first intentional Julia bridge gate registry after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#548 merged the mission-control dashboard and finish matrix after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#547 merged the q4 Julia REML bridge-forwarding fix after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
Follow-up reply posted: public R API is REML = TRUE/FALSE; native TMB is not yet a full REML fallback for the q4 phylogenetic location-scale model.
Dashboard branch repaired against current origin/main rather than the dirty detached checkout.
Posted the mission-control and next-gate start note to drmTMB#544.

blockers.item: R-first pivot: do not make the Julia route the only usable path for Ayumi-style q4 workflows.
engine = "tmb" is not yet a full REML fallback for Ayumi's bivariate q4 phylogenetic location-scale model.
Big ADEMP/power grids wait until smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass.

evidence.text: Source lives under docs/dev-log/dashboard, validates with tools/validate-mission-control.py, and serves from /tmp/drm-dashboard.
drmTMB#555 is the active Ayumi R-first workflow tracker; drmTMB#544 remains the bridge-gate epic after the native status path is clear.
Merged #565 makes Ayumi q4 Julia target inventory report fitted phylocov SDs, not placeholder 0.5 values; the admitted all-q4 bootstrap smoke returned 2/2 refits at 30 tips.

Merged DRM.jl main 9bdea65 verified through the drmTMB Ayumi harness: Julia ML and REML point fits return at 30 tips; ML bootstrap returns four q4 SD rows; REML bootstrap failure is now explicit and tracked.
Native TMB ML bootstrap smokes are negative evidence for fallback wording: 30-tip and 100-tip Ayumi runs returned bootstrap_unavailable after both bootstrap refits failed.
Native TMB careful optimizer settings rescue the 30-tip bootstrap smoke, but neither careful nor robust rescues the 100-tip smoke; the next claim needs per-refit diagnostics and retry evidence.
Current Ayumi reply boundary: Julia B=2 all-q4 bootstrap is plumbing evidence only; native bootstrap/profile intervals are not yet a usable q4 uncertainty fallback; no AI-REML or 10k speed claim.
HSquared scout confirms AI-REML wording belongs only to exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells; Ayumi's bivariate q4 sigma-phylo route still needs point/profile/bootstrap/speed evidence, not an AI-REML label.
Native R/TMB status rows now precede Julia speed work: #552/#553 banked q4 profile status, #557 banked REML rejection gates, and #559 banked the harness.
Rows separate engine, bridge, point estimate, Wald, profile, bootstrap, docs, visual, simulation, and release status.
Unsupported, planned, experimental, partial, covered, verified, banked, active, queued, blocked, and deferred are kept distinct.
